monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.purgeConfirmContent: {{count}} report(s) will be permanently deleted with all their vulnerabilities. This action is irreversible.
monitoring.purgeResult: Purge complete: {{reports}} report(s) and {{vulns}} vulnerability(ies) deleted.
monitoring.purgeableWarning: {{count}} report(s) exceed the retention period — purge recommended
monitoring.purgeImportConflict: Cannot purge while an import is in progress.

settings.staleDaysTooltip: Vulnerabilities not seen for this number of days will be marked as possibly stale.
settings.hideDaysTooltip: Vulnerabilities not seen for this number of days will be hidden by default.
settings.retentionMonthsTooltip: Reports older than this period will be automatically purged at application startup.

upgrade.title: Update Management
upgrade.launchNowConfirm: The update will start in 60 seconds. The application will be temporarily unavailable. Continue?
upgrade.resetStateConfirmContent: This will cancel any running or stuck upgrade. Use this if the app is stuck in 'Update in progress' mode after a restore or failure.

errors.UPGRADE_IN_PROGRESS: An upgrade is already in progress
errors.VERSION_NOT_NEWER: Version is not newer than current
errors.SCHEDULED_TIME_TOO_SOON: Scheduled time must be at least 15 minutes in the future

import.rescanContent: Watched directories will be re-scanned. Files already imported will be skipped. Missing files will be imported automatically.
import.rescanStarted: Rescan started — missing imports will arrive shortly
import.resetWarning: Warning: this action will delete ALL imported reports, vulnerabilities, and hosts. This action is irreversible.
import.deleteReport: Delete this report and all associated vulnerabilities?

user.newPassword: New password (leave empty to keep current)

rules.description: These rules define the default filters applied to all users.
rules.noTypeFilter: No type filter — all types are displayed.
rules.noCategorizationFilter: No categorization filter — all categorizations are displayed.
rules.noOsClassFilter: No OS class filter — all classes are displayed.
rules.freshnessDefault: Default freshness filter value applied on each user's first login.
rules.staleThresholdTooltip: Vulnerabilities not seen for this many days will be marked as potentially stale.
rules.hideThresholdTooltip: Vulnerabilities not seen for this many days will be hidden by default.

layer.categorizationDesc: Categorizations allow grouping vulnerabilities by infrastructure layer (OS, Middleware, Application, Network...).
layer.ruleDesc: Each rule associates a pattern (keyword) with a categorization. During import or reclassification, rules are evaluated by decreasing priority (first match wins).

branding.logoFormats: Accepted formats: SVG, PNG, JPG (max 500 KB). Recommended size: 200x50 pixels.
branding.footerHint: This text will appear in the footer. Leave empty to display "Qualys2Human".

ldap.title: LDAP / Active Directory connection
ldap.defaultAuthHelp: Pre-selected mode on the login page
ldap.tlsVerifyHint: Enable to verify server certificate (disable for self-signed certificates)
ldap.groupMappingDesc: Enter the full DN of the AD group for each profile. Priority: Administrator > User > Monitoring. If a user belongs to multiple groups, the highest profile will be assigned.

modals.sessionStayHint: Click "Stay logged in" to extend your session.

errors.ACCOUNT_LOCKED: Account locked. Please try again later.
errors.LDAP_NOT_ENABLED: Active Directory authentication is not enabled
errors.NO_MATCHING_AD_GROUP: No Active Directory group matches an authorized profile
errors.LOCAL_ACCOUNT_CONFLICT: A local account already exists with this name. Contact the administrator.
errors.INVALID_TIMEOUT_VALUE: Timeout must be at least 5 minutes
errors.INVALID_WARNING_VALUE: Warning must be at least 1 minute
errors.WARNING_EXCEEDS_TIMEOUT: Warning must be less than timeout
errors.WATCH_PATH_DUPLICATE: This directory is already being watched

proposals.duplicate: An identical proposal is already pending
proposals.cancelConfirmContent: This proposal will be permanently deleted.
proposals.empty: No proposals yet. You can propose categorization rules from the uncategorized QIDs page or vulnerability detail pages.
proposals.rejectReasonPlaceholder: Explain why this proposal was rejected (optional)...
proposals.duplicateWarning: An identical proposal is already pending
proposals.deleteConfirmContent: This proposal will be permanently deleted.
proposals.emptyState: No proposals yet. You can propose categorization rules from the uncategorized QIDs page or vulnerability detail pages.

help.selectSection: Select a section to display help.

overview.title: Dashboard
overview.intro: The dashboard displays a summary of the security status of your infrastructure.

kpi.title: Key Performance Indicators (KPI)
items: Total vulnerabilities — Total number of detected vulnerabilities.
Servers — Number of unique analyzed hosts.
Critical — Vulnerabilities with severity 4 and 5.
Average / host — Vulnerability-to-host ratio.

distributions.title: Distributions (triple donut)
items: Three side-by-side pie charts.
Severities — Distribution by severity level. Click to navigate to the filtered list.
OS class — Windows, NIX (Linux/Unix), Other. Click to navigate to the filtered host list.
Categorization — Distribution by categorization layer. Click to navigate to the filtered list.

widgets.title: Widgets
items: You can rearrange widgets by drag & drop.
Click "Default layout" to reset.
Your layout is saved automatically.

export.title: PDF Export
items: The PDF button generates a complete report including KPIs, charts, and tables with the custom logo.
Export works 100% offline.

vuln-detail.title: Vulnerability detail
vuln-detail.intro: This page displays the details of a specific vulnerability (QID). An orange banner identifies this view as the global QID card.

technical.title: Technical card
items: Structured card with all fields: QID, type, category, categorization, CVSS scores (base + temporal), CVE references, Bugtraq, affected hosts, and total occurrences.
Server-specific fields (status, port, detections…) are marked "N/A".

categorization.title: Categorization
items: Administrators can assign a categorization directly from the card via an inline selector.
The update is instant (optimistic) and applies to all occurrences of the QID.

descriptions.title: Threat / Impact / Solution
items: Detailed descriptions from Qualys, displayed in side-by-side cards.

servers.title: Affected servers
items: Table listing all affected hosts with an interactive detection status chart.
Click on a pie chart slice to filter the table.

export.title: Exports
items: CSV — Exports the server table.
PDF — Complete report with card, charts, and table.

host-detail.title: Host detail
host-detail.intro: This page displays host information and its list of vulnerabilities.

charts.title: Interactive charts
items: Severities — Vulnerability distribution by level. Click on a slice to filter the table.
Tracking methods — Distribution by detection method. Click to filter the table.
Both filters are cumulative. Tags appear above the table to indicate active filters.

table.title: Vulnerability table
items: Displays QID, title, severity, categorization, port, status, and last detection.
Click on a row to view the full detail (host + vulnerability).

export.title: Exports
items: CSV — Exports the vulnerability table.
PDF — Complete report with host card, charts, and table.

full-detail.title: Full detail
full-detail.intro: QID card for a specific server. A blue banner identifies the server (IP, DNS, OS). The layout is identical to the global QID card so you can find every piece of information in the same place.

technical.title: Technical card
items: Same structured card as the global QID card, with additional server-specific fields: status, port, protocol, FQDN, SSL, tracking method, detection dates, ticket, PCI, and scan results.

servers.title: Affected servers
items: The table of servers affected by this QID is displayed at the bottom of the page, just like on the global card.
The current server's row is highlighted in light blue for immediate identification.

trends.title: Trends
trends.intro: Analyze the time-based evolution of security metrics.

widgets.title: Available widgets
items: Avg vulns/host — Combo chart with average line and host count bars.
Host count — Evolution of scanned hosts over time.
Critical vulnerabilities — Trend line for severity 4 and 5 vulnerabilities.
Remediation rate — Percentage of fixed vulnerabilities per period.
Avg remediation time — Average number of days to fix a vulnerability.
Avg open vuln age — Average number of days since detection for unfixed vulnerabilities.
Breakdown by category — Multiple lines, one per categorization (layer).

controls.title: Controls
items: Period — 3 months, 6 months, 1 year, or custom.
Granularity — Day, week, or month.
Display — Show/hide each widget. Drag and drop to reorder.

export.title: Export
items: PDF export — Captures all visible charts into a PDF report.

methods.title: Import methods
items: Manual import — Click "Import a CSV" to upload a file. The import runs in the background: a progress banner appears at the top of the page and data refreshes automatically when complete.
Automatic import — Configure the file watcher to monitor directories and automatically import new CSV files.
History — View the status of each import (in progress, complete, error). The list updates automatically during imports.

watcher.title: Watched directories
items: Add directories to watch with a file pattern (e.g.: *.csv), optional recursive mode, and an exclusion date.
The watcher scans directories periodically and imports newly detected files.
Rescan sources — Forces a full re-scan of all directories. Useful after a database restore to reimport missing files.

admin-users.title: User management
admin-users.intro: Create, edit, and delete user accounts.

profiles.title: Profiles and accounts
items: Profiles — admin (full access), user (dashboard + export), monitoring (monitoring page only), or custom profile with granular permissions.
Deactivation — Disable an account without deleting it.
Password — Force a password change on next login.

session.title: Session settings
items: Configure the inactivity timeout and pre-expiration warning at the top of the page.
Default: 2-hour timeout, warning 5 minutes before.
A popup appears when the session is about to expire, with a countdown and the option to stay logged in or log out.
Closing all browser windows automatically invalidates the session.

lockout.title: Account lockout
items: After 5 failed attempts, the account is automatically locked for 15 minutes.
The "Status" column in the table displays a red "Locked" badge with the number of failed attempts.
An administrator can manually unlock an account via the lock button in the Actions column.

admin-rules.title: Enterprise rules
admin-rules.intro: Define the default filters applied to all users on their first login or via the "Enterprise rules" button in the filter bar.

filters.title: Configurable filters
items: Severities — Check the levels visible by default (1 to 5).
Types — Filter by vulnerability type (Vuln, Practice, Info). If no type is selected, all are displayed.
Categorization — Select the categorization layers to include by default.
OS class — Restrict by default to Windows, Linux/Unix, or both. If none is checked, all classes are displayed.
Default freshness — Initial freshness filter value for each user (Active, Possibly stale, All).

freshness.title: Freshness thresholds
items: Possibly stale — Number of days without detection before marking a vulnerability as potentially stale.
Hidden — Number of days without detection before hiding a vulnerability by default.

admin-layers.title: Categorization
admin-layers.intro: Organize your vulnerabilities into layers to classify them according to your business context.

layers.title: Layers
items: Create layers with a name, color, and priority order.
Layers appear in dashboard charts and vulnerability tables.

rules.title: Classification rules
items: Each layer can have automatic rules based on a field (title, category, type…) and a matching pattern.
Rules are applied in priority order.

reclassify.title: Reclassification
items: After modifying rules, click "Reclassify" to apply the new rules to all existing vulnerabilities.
Progress is displayed in real time.
Reclassification works by unique QID (1 QID = 1 categorization).

orphans.title: Orphan vulnerabilities
items: The "Categorize orphans" button displays a badge with the number of uncategorized QIDs.
For each QID, click "Create a rule" to open a pre-filled form with the vulnerability title.
A real-time counter shows how many orphan QIDs will be matched by the entered pattern.
You can create a new categorization directly from the form via "+ Create a categorization".
Once the rule is saved, matched QIDs disappear from the list.
After creating your rules, the "Run reclassification" button appears to apply the new rules.

admin-branding.title: Branding
admin-branding.intro: Customize the appearance of the application.

logo.title: Logo
items: Formats — SVG, PNG, JPG (max 500 KB).
Recommended size — 200x50 pixels.
Download the SVG template to create your logo.
The logo appears in PDF exports and on the login page.

footer.title: Footer
items: Customize the text displayed in the application footer.
Leave empty to display "Qualys2Human" by default.

banner.title: Announcement banner
items: Configure a banner displayed under the navigation menu to inform users (maintenance, update…).
Message — Displayed text (max 500 characters).
Color — Blue (info), yellow (warning), red (alert), or gray (other).
Visibility — Everyone, admins only, or disabled.
Users can dismiss the banner; it reappears on next login or if the message changes.

admin-ldap.title: LDAP / Active Directory
admin-ldap.intro: This page allows you to configure Active Directory authentication via LDAP.

connection.title: LDAP connection
items: Server URL — Domain controller address (ldap:// or ldaps://).
STARTTLS — Upgrades the connection to TLS on port 389 (not needed with ldaps://).
UPN template — User principal name format. Use {username} as a variable.
Search base — Base DN for searching users and groups (e.g.: DC=domain,DC=com).
Group attribute — LDAP attribute containing groups (default: memberOf).
TLS verification — Disable for self-signed certificates (air-gapped environments).

mapping.title: Group mapping
items: Associate an AD group DN with each Qualys2Human profile.
When a user logs in via AD, their groups are compared to the configured mappings.
The highest profile applies (Administrator > User > Monitoring).
If no group matches, login is denied.
AD accounts are automatically created in the database on first login.

test.title: Connection test
items: The "Test connection" button performs a direct bind with the provided credentials.
This verifies that the URL, UPN template, and TLS settings are correct before enabling AD authentication for all users.

monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.intro: Monitor the health of the application and server.

services.title: Services and resources
items: Services — PostgreSQL and API status.
Resources — CPU, memory, and disk with alert thresholds.
DB pool — Active and available connections.
Alerts — Automatic notifications when a threshold is exceeded (CPU/RAM/disk > 80% = warning, > 95% = critical).

refresh.title: Auto-refresh
items: The page refreshes automatically every 30 seconds.

retention.title: Data retention
items: Reports older than the retention period (configurable in Admin > Settings) are automatically purged at startup.
If reports exceed the retention period, a banner appears with a manual purge button (admin only).

investigation.title: Investigation — Search
investigation.intro: The Search page allows you to quickly find a vulnerability or a server.

vulnSearch.title: Vulnerability search
items: Enter a QID (number) for an exact search, or a keyword to search titles.
If only one result matches, navigation to the detail card is automatic.

hostSearch.title: Server search
items: Enter an IP address, DNS name, or NetBIOS name (even partial).
The search is performed on all three fields simultaneously.

results.title: Results
items: Results are displayed in a table. Click on a row to access the detailed card.
Maximum 200 results displayed.

filters.title: Filters
filters.intro: The filter bar allows you to restrict the data displayed on all pages.

dimensions.title: Filter dimensions
items: Severity — Check the levels to display (1=Minimal to 5=Urgent).
Type — Filter by type (Vuln, Practice, Info).
Categorization — Filter by categorization layer.
OS class — Filter by operating system (Windows, NIX).
Freshness — Active, possibly stale, or all.
Dates — Restrict to an import date range.

actions.title: Quick actions
items: Reset — Clears all filters.
Enterprise rules — Reapplies the filters defined by the administrator.
Presets — Save and load filter combinations.

persistence.title: Persistence
items: Filters are applied in real time and saved automatically per user.

proposals.title: Rule Proposals
proposals.intro: Propose categorization rules for admin review. Your proposals help organize uncategorized vulnerabilities.

overview.title: How it works
items: Any user can propose a categorization rule from uncategorized QIDs or vulnerability details
Admins review, modify if needed, and approve or reject proposals
Approved proposals become active rules — admin triggers reclassification manually

propose.title: Proposing a rule
items: Click 'Propose' on any uncategorized QID
Choose a pattern (keyword), target field (title or category), and category
The match count shows how many QIDs your rule would affect

review.title: Admin review
items: Pending proposals appear at the top of both the Uncategorized QIDs page and the Rules page
Admins can approve directly, modify then approve, or reject with a comment
Once all proposals are reviewed, admin triggers reclassification manually

status.title: Tracking proposals
items: View all your proposals from 'My Proposals' in the user menu
Statuses: Pending (under review), Approved (rule created), Rejected (with admin feedback)
You can cancel pending proposals before they are reviewed

orphans.title: Uncategorized QIDs
orphans.intro: This page lists vulnerabilities that haven't been assigned to any categorization. Use filters to narrow the list, then propose or create classification rules.

filters.title: Filters and search
items: Severity and type filters are initialized from the enterprise configuration (same as Overview)
Use the search bar to filter by vulnerability title or category
The counter at the top shows filtered QIDs vs. total count

propose.title: Proposing a rule (user)
items: Click 'Propose' next to a QID to submit a suggestion to the administrator
Choose the pattern, target field, and desired categorization
The match counter helps you assess the impact of your proposal

admin.title: Admin management
items: Admins see a pending proposals panel at the top of the page
They can approve, modify then approve, or reject each proposal
The 'Create rule' button allows directly creating a rule without going through a proposal

reclassify.title: Reclassification
items: After creating or approving rules, a 'Launch reclassification' button appears
Reclassification is deferred: process all proposals first, then launch once
Reclassification progress is visible from the Categorization page (admin)

admin-upgrade.title: Update Management
admin-upgrade.intro: This page lets you manage application updates: upload a package, schedule installation, and view history.

upload.title: Upload
items: Select the .zip update package file and its .sig signature file
The upload is split into 2 MB chunks for reliable transfer
A progress bar shows upload progress

validation.title: Validation
items: After upload, the package is automatically validated: ZIP integrity, Ed25519 signature, version
The target version must be newer than the current version
A summary shows the result of each check with green/red icons

schedule.title: Scheduling
items: Choose a date and time for the update (minimum 15 minutes in the future)
Configure notification thresholds to warn users before maintenance
The 'Launch now' button starts the update in 60 seconds after confirmation

history.title: History
items: View the 50 most recent updates with their status, duration, and any errors
Possible statuses: Completed (green), Failed (red), Rolled back (orange), Cancelled (grey)
Reset state — If the app is stuck in 'Update in progress' mode (e.g. after a database restore), the red button forces a state reset.

maintenance.warning: Maintenance scheduled at {{time}} (in {{countdown}}) -- please save your work
